utilization_high.title: Pool utilization high
steps: Confirm utilization on the dashboard and in /metrics (meridian_pool_utilization_ratio).
Check whether a single borrower is driving the draw via /accounts and /positions.
Near 100% utilization, withdrawals are liquidity-capped; communicate this to lenders.
If utilization stays elevated, consider adjusting the interest-rate curve via governance.

accounts_near_floor.title: Accounts approaching the liquidation floor
steps: Identify the affected accounts from the margin engine /risk output.
Confirm the keeper is running and reading health (keeper logs, dry-run off in production).
Verify oracle prices are fresh; a stale feed can distort health factors.
No action is required if health is recovering; escalate only if accounts cross the floor.

accounts_liquidatable.title: Accounts below the liquidation floor
steps: Confirm the keeper has submitted liquidations (keeper logs, on-chain Liquidate events).
If none submitted, check keeper RPC health, the signing key balance, and KEEPER_DRY_RUN.
As a fallback, submit LiquidationModule.liquidate(account) from an authorized keeper key.
Confirm the pool was made whole after liquidation via the indexer and /pools.

new_liquidations.title: New liquidations observed
steps: Review the liquidations in the indexer (/liquidations) and on-chain receipts.
Confirm each made the pool whole and seized collateral as expected.
Correlate with the price move or utilization spike that triggered them.
No action needed for routine liquidations; investigate clusters or bad debt.

snapshot_stale.title: Indexer snapshot stale
steps: Check the indexer process and logs; confirm it is still polling the chain tip.
Verify the indexer RPC endpoint is reachable and not rate-limited.
Confirm INDEXER_SNAPSHOT_PATH is writable and shared with the alerts service.
Restart the indexer if needed; it resumes from the last fully-scanned block.
